title: Skill inventory and agent steering review
goal: Review whether the shipped and installed skill inventory is complete, current, discoverable, and strong enough to steer agent behavior before prose fallback.
scope: Root workspace installed skills and payload copies
Planning installed/package skills and skill discovery
Repo-local maintainer skills under tools/skills and memory repo skills
Skill catalog discovery and recommendation output
non_goals: Audit every skill sentence for copyediting

review_mode.review question: Does the current skill inventory make the intended Agentic Workspace behavior visible and hard to skip across shipped surfaces?
review_mode.inputs inspected first: agentic-workspace start, agentic-workspace skills, all REGISTRY.json files, all SKILL.md files under .agentic-workspace, packages, src payload, and tools

review_method.commands used: uv run agentic-workspace start --target . --format json; uv run agentic-workspace skills --target . --format json; rg --files -g REGISTRY.json -g SKILL.md; uv run python registry/skill coverage scripts; rg for bootstrap naming and workflow trigger terms
review_method.evidence sources: Static inspection of current shipped surfaces, installed repo copies, package payload copies, registry coverage, and prior friction context around agents skipping or misusing package workflow

references.label: Temporary memory bootstrap skill payload list
Repo memory skill with stale bootstrap commands
Discover temporary Memory bootstrap skills in skill inventory
Refresh skill metadata to prefer non-bootstrap CLI names
Add skill coverage for high-risk workflow decisions

findings.title: Temporary Memory bootstrap skills are shipped but not registered or discoverable as skills
findings.summary: The Memory package ships temporary install and cleanup skills under `.agentic-workspace/memory/bootstrap/skills/`, and prompts explicitly tell agents to use that path, but there is no registry for that temporary skill surface and `agentic-workspace skills` does not discover it.
findings.evidence: `packages/memory/src/repo_memory_bootstrap/_installer_shared.py` includes `.agentic-workspace/memory/bootstrap/skills/install/SKILL.md` and `cleanup/SKILL.md` in `BOOTSTRAP_WORKSPACE_FILES`, while the skill catalog sources in `src/agentic_workspace/cli.py` only include workspace-core, planning-bundled, memory-core, repo-memory, and repo-tools registries. A registry coverage scan found `packages/memory/bootstrap/.agentic-workspace/memory/bootstrap/skills/install/SKILL.md` and `cleanup/SKILL.md` as unregistered shipped skill files.
findings.risk if unchanged: The install/adopt flow still depends on a prose prompt naming a path instead of skill discovery. Weak agents may miss or underuse the temporary skills exactly when bootstrap review needs conservative behavior.
findings.suggested action: Add a registry for the temporary Memory bootstrap workspace or teach the workspace skills command to discover that explicit temporary skill surface when present. Prefer the non-bootstrap `agentic-memory` command names in those temporary skills at the same time.
findings.promotion target: GitHub issue or roadmap candidate for Memory bootstrap skill discoverability
findings.promotion trigger: Before further bootstrap/adopt UX evaluation or model-harness testing of clean installs

findings.title: Skill metadata and bodies still steer agents toward historical bootstrap names
findings.summary: The normal CLIs now have preferred `agentic-planning` and `agentic-memory` aliases, but several skill registries and skill bodies still present bootstrap as the main identity rather than a compatibility alias.
findings.evidence: The Planning installed/package registries still use owner `agentic-planning-bootstrap`, skill id `bootstrap-upgrade`, and phrases such as `upgrade planning bootstrap`. Memory registries and skills still use owner `agentic-memory-bootstrap` and phrases such as `memory bootstrap`; `.agentic-workspace/memory/repo/skills/memory-reporting/SKILL.md` still starts with `uv run agentic-memory-bootstrap status` and `doctor`. Package Memory temporary install/cleanup skills also still say `agentic-memory-bootstrap init` and `bootstrap-cleanup`.
findings.risk if unchanged: The skill-first path can reintroduce the same naming confusion the CLI alias work reduced: agents may infer these are install-only bootstrap tools, or copy stale command names even when the preferred names exist.
findings.suggested action: Run a skill-specific alias refresh: update ordinary skill text and activation hints to prefer `agentic-planning` and `agentic-memory`, reserve `*-bootstrap` for compatibility notes or package-name contexts, and consider renaming lifecycle skill ids away from generic `bootstrap-upgrade` where the task is ongoing module maintenance.
findings.promotion target: GitHub issue or roadmap candidate for skill copy/metadata alias refresh
findings.promotion trigger: Before treating the skill inventory as the primary agent-steering surface

findings.title: The skill arsenal does not yet cover the highest-risk workflow decisions
findings.summary: There is a workspace startup skill, a planning autopilot skill, and an orchestrator skill, but no first-class skills for progressive maturity decomposition, adaptive assurance/classification, execplan creation/tightening, closeout distillation, bypass/trust review, or issue/queue promotion. Those are exactly the decisions agents have been missing or misapplying.
findings.evidence: `workspace-startup` routes startup, config, proof, and closeout in 31 lines but does not carry the decomposition or assurance workflow. `planning-autopilot` explicitly says to use it only when active work is already bounded and validation is obvious. `planning-orchestrator-workflow` handles delegation after planning exists. The inventory lacks corresponding shipped skills for the pre-execplan path: classify shape, decompose epic/lane work, promote into a valid plan, apply assurance/delegation posture, and close out by distilling residue.
findings.risk if unchanged: Skill-first routing will still work best after a plan exists, while the main failure mode remains before implementation: agents jump from prompt to coding, freehand invalid plans, skip classification, or miss closeout trust decisions.
findings.suggested action: Add a small set of workflow-critical skills that match the real decision points: `workspace-work-shape`, `planning-decompose`, `planning-new-plan-tighten`, `planning-closeout`, and `workspace-closeout-trust` or equivalent. Each should primarily call compact CLI commands and stop before broad prose.
findings.promotion target: GitHub issue or roadmap candidate for skill-first workflow coverage
findings.promotion trigger: Before the next model-evaluation optimization loop focused on agents honoring config and workflow obligations

recommendation.promote: Promote all three findings. The first is an inventory/discoverability bug, the second is consistency debt that undermines the recent alias work, and the third is the main product opportunity for a skill-first agent-steering model.
recommendation.defer: Defer repo-local maintainer skill cleanup unless those skills become part of shipped host-repo behavior; they are visible but source_kind marks them repo-owned.
recommendation.dismiss: Do not treat the presence of duplicate installed/package skill copies as a problem by itself; source and installed payload copies are expected as long as maintainer-surface checks keep them synchronized.

retention.trigger: After promoted issues or roadmap candidates capture the actionable work
retention.proof surface: GitHub issues or planning state plus future skill inventory checks
retention.source retention rule: Store decision, confidence, source refs, and reopen/promote triggers; do not copy issue titles, current status snapshots, long command output, or source summaries unless the copied text is the judgment itself.
retention.distillation path: When decisions are promoted into issues, planning state, Memory, docs, contracts, or checks, keep this artifact as evidence/history and remove or summarize reconstructable source context at the next cleanup.

review_finding.rule: Use only these headings when a prose finding is needed; keep the canonical fields structured.

field_map.Next owner: execution_summary/follow-on routed to or required_continuation/owner surface

handoff_or_closeout.rule: Prefer structured fields; use this shape only for short human-readable summaries.

validation_commands: rg --files -g REGISTRY.json -g SKILL.md .agentic-workspace packages src tools
rg -n "bootstrap|agentic-planning-bootstrap|agentic-memory-bootstrap" .agentic-workspace packages tools src/agentic_workspace/_payload -g SKILL.md -g REGISTRY.json
drift_log: 2026-05-05: Review created from a skill-system pass over shipped and installed skill surfaces.
